floor_history.headline: fable-blitz recon: compressed animation-frame bitstream decoder over D_800F1B18[idx*0x570]; the ENTIRE bit-read idiom (refill/simple GETBITS) is already byte-match-proven in pure C by the adjacent COM

frontier.hypothesis: A full-body first draft translating the mapped skeleton, reusing func_8001979C's proven GETBITS spelling verbatim at every read site (open-coded, macro-expanded), collapses the 747 distance to a small residual in ONE step.
frontier.mechanism: cse.c + combine.c produce the exact subu/srlv/sllv/or instruction forms already byte-proven for this spelling in this same TU (func_8001979C matched); expr.c open-codes each expansion so no jump.c cross-jump interference; synth_mult chains for *0x570 and *0x118 are the default expand_mult decompositions (both confirmed byte-identical in the sibling).
frontier.next_probe: Write the draft into the empty stub at src/code6cac.c:930, run `& tools/wteng.ps1 main sandbox func_800198D0 --disable all`, record the masked distance and diff-classify the residual.

frontier.hypothesis: Register binding (buf=$t0, bitsLeft=$t1, wordPtr=$t3, slot=$s0, frame=$s1, out=$s2) reproduces WITHOUT pins from natural declaration/use order: s1/s2 are the incoming a1/a2 values live across the whole body (callee-saved forced in leaf because t-regs are exhausted by the decoder state), and the three reader-state pseudos have the highest n_refs so global.c hands them the first free caller-saved regs.
frontier.mechanism: local-alloc.c/global.c allocno priority = ref count x loop depth; the reader state is referenced in every GETBITS expansion (~20 sites, most inside the depth-2 channel loop), dominating all other pseudos; no calls -> caller-saved regs are free for the taking.
frontier.next_probe: After draft compiles, read the sandbox masked-vs-unmasked gap (masked 0 with reg diffs = pure RA work); if reg classes are wrong, reorder local declarations / split scopes per .claude/rules/register-alloc-pure-c before considering anything else.

frontier.hypothesis: The keyframe path needs NO goto: writing it as straight-line code that falls into the shared `for (sub = start; sub < (frame&7); sub++)` loop (start=0 for keyframe, start=(frame&7)-1 for delta path) reproduces the j .L80019DB8 / j .L80019D44 layout, and the `if (sub == 0) first-order else second-order` accumulation if/else inside the channel loop reproduces s:737-752.
frontier.mechanism: stmt.c/jump.c natural layout: the common loop is emitted once after the if/else region; each arm's exit jump to the shared loop head is exactly the target's j+delay-slot (t8 init in the delay slot at s:314/348 = the arm's last assignment before the join).
frontier.next_probe: In the first sandbox run, check the control-flow class of `diagnose func_800198D0` -- if control-flow divergence appears at the join, try arm-ordering (delta path first vs last) before any goto; only fall back to `goto` shared-label per .claude/rules/shared-end-label if both orders fail.
